# Azion Docs — devtools

> All pages of the devtools section. Root index: https://www.azion.com/en/llms.txt

- [Dev Tools](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/): Discover tools that help you take your applications one step further and find more information such as reference documentation, guides, and tutorials. These developer tools — including the API, GraphQL API, Azion Lib, CLI, SDKs, the Terraform Provider, and the MCP server — help you build, automate, and manage your Azion applications end to end, from local development to production.
- [Get started with Azion API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/api/): Learn more about official endpoints, methods, and API use cases of the Azion RESTful API.
- [API v4 Migration](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/api/v4-migration/): Find detailed information about Azions plan for migration to our new API v4.
- [Azion CLI](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/): Explore Azion CLI for edge computing manage and deploy applications, integrate with Azion APIs, and optimize with Go SDK.
- [Azion IaC](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/azion-config-js/): Learn how to customize your application's configurations using the azion.config.js file. This documentation provides detailed explanations of the configuration properties.
- [Azion CLI build](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/build/): Learn how to build and deploy applications effectively using commands like azion build with support for multiple frameworks and configurations.
- [Azion CLI clone](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/clone/): Learn how to clone Applications using Azion CLI for quick replication of edge applications.
- [Azion CLI config](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/config/): Manage Azion configuration resources from manifest files using the config command with init, apply, and delete subcommands.
- [Azion CLI init, build, and deploy commands](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/create-application/): Learn to initialize, build, and deploy applications using Azion's Edge Platform with interactive CLI commands and comprehensive deployment options.
- [Azion CLI create](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/create/): Learn how to utilize Azion CLI for creating and managing Applications, Functions, and other resources efficiently to enhance your edge solutions.
- [Azion CLI delete](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/delete/): Learn to manage your Edge resources using Azion CLI, including deleting applications, functions, and setting up cache rules effectively.
- [Azion CLI deploy](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/deploy/): Learn how to deploy your applications using Azion CLI with a guide on usage, commands and options for deploying onto the edge network.
- [Azion CLI describe](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/describe/): Explore detailed command usage for managing applications, functions, domains, and more with Azion CLI.
- [Azion CLI dev](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/dev-command/): Discover how to build and run Azion Applications locally using the dev command. Learn to link projects and use Functions effectively.
- [Azion CLI global options](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/globals/): Explore essential Azion CLI commands for managing configurations, tokens, and debugging. Helpful guide for version checks, log settings, and more.
- [Azion CLI init](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/init/): Learn how to initialize an application using Azion's diverse templates, supporting frameworks like Next.js, Vue, React, and Angular.
- [Azion CLI link](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/link-command/): Learn how to link and deploy projects on the Azion Web Platform, using interactive commands for optimal edge computing integration.
- [Azion CLI list](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/list/): List your applications, functions and other resources through Azion CLI.
- [Azion CLI login](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/login/): Log in to your Azion account and save a personal token locally to authorize CLI commands.
- [Azion CLI logout](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/logout/): Log out of your Azion account and remove the personal token saved locally.
- [Azion CLI logs](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/logs/): Watch your functions and application's HTTP events logs through Azion CLI.
- [Azion CLI profiles](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/profiles/): Manage your Azion CLI profiles to switch between different accounts and configurations easily.
- [Azion CLI purge](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/purge/): Azion CLI purge allows you to instantly delete the cache of your content at Azion.
- [Azion CLI reset](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/reset/): Reset your settings to default and delete personal token if possible through Azion CLI.
- [Azion CLI rollback](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/rollback/): Rollback to a previous deployment version by restoring static files from a prior deploy using Azion CLI.
- [Azion CLI sync](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/sync/): Synchronize your local file containing your existing application resources configuration with remote resources through Azion CLI.
- [Azion CLI unlink](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/unlink-command/): Unlink an existing project from Azion Web Platform.
- [Azion CLI update](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/update/): Update your applications, functions and other resources through Azion CLI.
- [Azion CLI warmup](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/warmup/): Preload URLs into edge cache using cache warming to improve performance for your applications with Azion CLI.
- [Azion CLI whoami](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/cli/reference/whoami/): Azion CLI whoami command displays the email of the user currently logged in.
- [Console Kit](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/console-kit/): Developed with Vue/Vite, PrimeVue, and Tailwind, Azion Console Kit is a front-end development kit used for crafting a customized Azion Console interface.
- [Form Builder](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/form-builder/): Build visual, schema-driven forms to configure Azion Functions arguments in Applications and Firewalls.
- [GraphQL API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/): Request the data you want and receive nothing more, nothing less. The Azion GraphQL API lets you query metrics, events, and accounting data with precise, flexible requests, retrieving exactly the fields you need in a single call instead of over-fetching or under-fetching information.
- [How to query aggregated data with GraphQL API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/guides/graphql-aggregated-data/): Aggregated data can also be considered as structured data that have been clustered — they go through modifications to allow a better analytic processing seeking a segmented analysis.
- [How to query metadata with GraphQL API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/guides/graphql-metadata/): Unlock the power of GraphQL API metadata with this guide to understand the relationship and the utility of the request processes using the available datasets.
- [How to select Top X queries with GraphQL API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/guides/graphql-top-x-query/): Top X queries are mainly used for performing queries and analysis on data. They help visualize how resources and tools are being used and provide a more detailed analysis of certain conditions that are either more or less commonly used.
- [How to query usage data from Application](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/guides/query-applications-usage-data-with-graphql/): This guide explains how to query data from the Consumption dataset and check the Application's data usage using the GraphiQL Playground.
- [Query Top URLs Impacted by Bots with GraphQL](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/guides/query-bot-manager-breakdown-data-with-graphql/): This guide explains how to query the top 5 URLs impacted by bot activity using the GraphiQL playground and the botManagerBreakdownMetrics dataset.
- [How to query Bot Manager data with GraphQL API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/guides/query-bot-manager-data-with-graphql/): This guide will explain how to query data from the botManagerMetrics dataset using the GraphiQL playground.
- [How to query Connected Users data with GraphQL API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/guides/query-connected-users-data-with-graphql/): This guide will explain how to query data from the connectedUsersMetrics dataset using the GraphiQL playground.
- [How to query usage data from Data Stream](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/guides/query-data-stream-usage-data-with-graphql/): This guide explains how to query data from the Consumption dataset and check usage related to Data Stream using the GraphiQL Playground.
- [How to query usage data from Functions](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/guides/query-functions-usage-data-with-graphql/): This guide explains how to query data from the Consumption dataset and check Functions' compute time and invocations using the GraphiQL Playground.
- [How to query GraphQL requests on Postman](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/guides/query-graphql-postman/): You can query GraphQL requests directly on Postman and receive responses.
- [Query the httpBreakdownMetrics Dataset](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/guides/query-httpbreakdownmetrics-data-with-graphql/): This guide will explain how to query data from the httpBreakdownMetrics dataset using the GraphiQL playground.
- [How to query usage data from Image Processor](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/guides/query-image-processor-usage-data-with-graphql/): This guide explains how to query data from the Consumption dataset and check usage related to Image Processor using the GraphiQL Playground.
- [How to query usage data from Tiered Cache](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/guides/query-tiered-cache-usage-data-with-graphql/): This guide explains how to query data from the Consumption dataset and check usage related to Tiered Cache using the GraphiQL Playground.
- [How to identify the top attacks using GraphQL API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/guides/query-top-attacks-with-graphql/): This guide will explain how to filter the top attack types, ranked by occurrence, as identified by the WAF.
- [Identify Top Attack-Traffic IPs with GraphQL](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/guides/query-top-ips-attack-traffic-with-graphql/): This guide will explain how to filter the IPs that generated the most requests identified by the WAF as attacks.
- [Error Responses GraphQL API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/reference/error-responses/): The GraphQL API returns standard HTTP status codes to indicate an error. Whenever you receive a status code while trying to run a request, you'll also receive an error message.
- [Features GraphQL API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/reference/features/): Explore the capabilities of Azion GraphQL API with features like datasets, real-time metrics, events handling, and advanced data querying options like filtering, sorting, and pagination.
- [First Steps GraphQL API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/reference/first-steps/): Learn how to access Azion GraphQL API and the GraphQL playground.
- [Accounting GraphQL API Fields](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/reference/gql-accounting-fields/): Explore detailed fields and descriptions for the Accounting GraphQL API dataset on Azion, including client IDs, product metrics, and financial data.
- [Billing GraphQL API Fields](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/reference/gql-billing-fields/): See descriptions for all fields from each Billing GraphQL API dataset.
- [Consumption GraphQL API Fields](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/reference/gql-consumption-fields/): Explore detailed fields and descriptions for the Consumption GraphQL API dataset on Azion, including usage data for Azion products.
- [Real-Time Events GraphQL API Fields](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/reference/gql-real-time-events-fields/): Explore detailed field descriptions for each dataset within Azion's Real-Time Events GraphQL API, aiding in advanced data querying and API usage.
- [Real-Time Metrics GraphQL API Fields](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/reference/gql-real-time-metrics-fields/): Explore comprehensive insights on Real-Time Metrics and GraphQL API datasets with detailed field descriptions for Applications on Azion Console.
- [Limits GraphQL API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/reference/limits/): Explore the essential limits of the GraphQL API including rows per query and fields limit, ensuring optimal performance and understanding error responses.
- [GraphQL API Overview](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/reference/overview/): Graphic Query Language (GraphQL) is a language created for use with APIs. It works as an alternative to REST APIs, allowing you to get data according to your needs. GraphQL gives you autonomy to request the data you want and receive exactly what you asked for through a JSON result.
- [GraphiQL Playground](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/reference/playground/): Use the GraphiQL playground to explore and understand how it works.
- [Queries examples](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/reference/queries-examples/): Explore Azion's dedicated repository for GraphQL API query examples. Find updated query samples for Data Stream, Applications, and more on GitHub.
- [Queries GraphQL API](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/graphql/reference/queries/): Explore how to effectively use GraphQL API for real-time, aggregated, and financial data querying with examples, and understand limits and optimization techniques.
- [Customize Azion Console with Console Kit](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/guides/customize-interface-console-kit/): Azion Console Kit allows you to craft a custom Azion interface to fit your business needs. In this guide, you'll learn how to make some simple visual changes to the Azion Console.
- [Integrate Azion Console & API with Console Kit](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/guides/integrate-with-api-console-kit/): Azion Console Kit allows you to craft a custom Azion interface to fit your business needs. In this guide, you'll learn how to show more information from the Azion API on the Console.
- [Azion Libraries overview](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/lib/): Learn about Azion Libraries, a suite of tools for interacting with Azion services, products, and utilities.
- [Azion AI Client module](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/lib/reference/ai-client/): Configure a specialized AI to help you with Azion products.
- [Azion Application module](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/lib/reference/application/): Manage Applications using the Application module.
- [Azion `Client` interface](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/lib/reference/client/): Interact with Azion products and services through Azion Libraries' Client interface.
- [Azion `Config` module](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/lib/reference/config/): Configure and validate options for Applications using the Config module.
- [Azion `Cookies` library](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/lib/reference/cookies/): Learn how to use the Azion `Cookies` library to handle cookies in web applications.
- [Azion `Domains` library](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/lib/reference/domains/): Learn how to use the Azion `Domains` library to manage domains in applications.
- [Azion `JWT` library](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/lib/reference/jwt/): Learn how to use the Azion `JWT` library to manage JSON Web Tokens in your web applications.
- [Azion KV library](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/lib/reference/kv/): Learn how to use the KV library to interact with the KV Store using a Redis-like API.
- [Azion `Purge` library](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/lib/reference/purge/): Learn how to use the `Purge` library to manage cache purging in your web applications.
- [Azion SQL library](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/lib/reference/sql/): Learn how to use the SQL library to interact with the SQL Database API.
- [Azion `Storage` library](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/lib/reference/storage/): Learn how to use the `Storage` library to interact with the Object Storage API.
- [Azion `Types` package](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/lib/reference/types/): Explore the `Types` package for global TypeScript types across the Azion platform.
- [Azion unenv Preset](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/lib/reference/unenv/): Use unenv polyfills for Node.js compatibility with Azion Runtime.
- [Azion `Utils` package](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/lib/reference/utils/): Learn about the Utils package, offering utility functions for Azion Functions.
- [WASM Image Processor library](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/lib/reference/wasm-image-processor/): Explore the `WASM Image Processor` library for efficient image processing using WebAssembly.
- [Model Context Protocol (MCP)](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/mcp/): Connect AI code assistants to Azion Platform through the Model Context Protocol, enabling intelligent assistance for deployment, configuration, and management tasks.
- [How to run an MCP server on Azion](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/mcp/guides/run-mcp-server/): Learn how to configure an MCP server on azion
- [MCP Cache Testing Guide](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/mcp/reference/cache-testing/): Comprehensive guide for testing, debugging, and optimizing cache behavior on Azion Platform using MCP tools and cURL commands.
- [MCP Configuration Guide](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/mcp/reference/configuration/): Step-by-step instructions to configure Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration with various code assistants and development tools.
- [MCP Examples](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/mcp/reference/examples/): Practical examples and workflows for using Model Context Protocol (MCP) with AI code assistants to streamline Azion Platform development.
- [How MCP Works](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/mcp/reference/how-it-works/): Understand the technical architecture behind Azion's Model Context Protocol and learn how to implement your own MCP server using modern technologies.
- [MCP Local Development](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/mcp/reference/local-development/): Set up a local development environment for Azion MCP server, including cloning, building, and deploying your own instance.
- [MCP Troubleshooting](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/mcp/reference/troubleshooting/): Solutions for common issues when configuring and using Model Context Protocol (MCP) with Azion Platform and code assistants.
- [Azion Terraform Provider](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/terraform/): Azion Terraform Provider v2.0 is an open source project that enables infrastructure as code management for Azion API v4. Manage workloads, connectors, applications, and more through Terraform configurations.
- [Migrating from Terraform Provider v1.x to v2.0](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/terraform/guides/migrate-v1-to-v2/): Complete migration guide for upgrading from Azion Terraform Provider v1.x (API v3) to v2.0 (API v4). Includes breaking changes, resource mapping, and configuration examples.
- [Applications - Terraform Resources](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/terraform/reference/applications/): Manage Azion Applications with Terraform. Configure cache, rules engine, device groups and function instances using azion_application resources.
- [Best Practices - Terraform Provider](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/terraform/reference/best-practices/): Learn best practices for using the Azion Terraform Provider. Organize your code, manage states, and implement CI/CD efficiently.
- [Certificates - Terraform Resources](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/terraform/reference/certificates/): Manage Azion digital certificates with Terraform. Configure SSL/TLS certificates for your applications using azion_digital_certificate.
- [Connectors - Terraform Resources](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/terraform/reference/connectors/): Manage Azion connectors with Terraform. Learn how to create and configure origin connectors using azion_connector for your applications.
- [Edge DNS - Terraform Resources](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/terraform/reference/dns/): Manage Azion DNS with Terraform. Configure DNS zones, records, and DNSSEC using azion_intelligent_dns resources.
- [Resources & Data Sources - Terraform Provider](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/terraform/reference/examples/): Practical examples for using the Azion Terraform Provider v2.0. Learn how to use Resources and Data Sources to manage your infrastructure as code.
- [Quick Start Guide - Terraform Provider](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/terraform/reference/getting-started/): Get started with the Azion Terraform Provider in minutes. This guide shows you how to configure the provider, create basic resources, and apply your first infrastructure as code.
- [Security - Terraform Resources](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/terraform/reference/security/): Manage Azion security resources with Terraform. Configure firewall, WAF, network lists, and more using azion_firewall resources.
- [Migrating from Terraform Provider v1.x to v2.0](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/terraform/reference/terraform-migration-v3-to-v4/): Complete migration guide for upgrading from Azion Terraform Provider v1.x (API v3) to v2.0 (API v4). Includes breaking changes, resource mapping, and configuration examples.
- [Workloads - Terraform Resources](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/terraform/reference/workloads/): Manage Azion workloads with Terraform. Learn how to create, update, and manage workload deployments using azion_workload and azion_workload_deployment.
- [Azion Terraform Provider v1.x (API v3)](https://www.azion.com/en/documentation/devtools/terraform/v1/): Legacy documentation for Azion Terraform Provider v1.x, compatible with Azion API v3. For new projects, use Terraform Provider v2.0 with API v4.
